ai_strategy_lead.name: Joseph T. Yun
ai_strategy_lead.title: Director of Artificial Intelligence Enablement, Pitt Digital / Research Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering

Generative AI @ Pitt — enterprise tool landing page
Report of the Ad Hoc Committee on Gen AI in Research & Education
Hub for AI and Data Science Leadership (HAIL) launch announcement

ai_strategy.summary: Pitt articulates its AI posture through a cluster of interlocking institutional programs rather than a single strategy document. The centerpiece is HAIL (Hub for AI and Data Science Leadership), inaugurated December 2025 as a 'practical-first' engine to unify AI research, teaching, enterprise tools, and regional workforce development under a 'Responsible Data Science' framing. A March 2024 Ad Hoc Committee report from the Offices of the Provost and the Senior Vice Chancellor for Research established the 'Points to Consider' living policy framework, and the AI at Pitt portal coordinates clinical AI (CPACE), computational infrastructure (PSC, CRCD), generative AI tooling (PittGPT, Claude for Education, Gemini, Copilot), and community engagement through the Pitt AI Scholar-Teacher Alliance (PASTA).

key_provisions: Two-tier tool classification: University GenAI Tools (PittGPT, Claude for Education, Gemini, Copilot Chat, NotebookLM) approved for Protected/Restricted data; Public GenAI Tools (ChatGPT, DeepSeek, etc.) limited to public data only
Users accountable for verifying accuracy of all GenAI outputs and for safeguarding personal information and IP
Prohibitions on impersonation, falsification of research data, disinformation, and harassment
Tools must clear Vendor Security Risk Assessment before use with University data
Noncompliance may trigger disciplinary action, suspension, or termination of access under Faculty/Staff Handbooks, Student Code, or Collective Bargaining Agreements
Teaching Center publishes sample syllabus language spanning restrictive-to-AI-integrated options; faculty set course-level expectations with institutional guidance
ai_policy.summary: Pitt's Acceptable Use of Generative AI Tools policy is a comprehensive, data-classification-driven framework that pairs a permissive stance on voluntary faculty adoption with strict guardrails around sensitive data. Private Pitt-hosted tools (PittGPT) are required for restricted data while public tools remain allowed for public data only, and the Teaching Center's resources give faculty a spectrum of syllabus-level options rather than mandating a single rule across courses.

bodies.name: Hub for AI and Data Science Leadership (HAIL)
bodies.scope: University-wide hub coordinating AI research, education, enterprise tools, and regional workforce development; launched December 2025 under Director Michael Colaresi

bodies.name: Ad Hoc Committee on Gen AI in Research and Education
bodies.scope: Combined Provost and Senior Vice Chancellor for Research committee that produced Pitt's March 2024 'Considerations for Responsible Use and Recommendations for Generative AI' report; co-chaired by John Radzilowicz and Lisa Parker

bodies.name: Generative AI Committee (Teaching & Learning)
bodies.scope: Standing committee coordinating faculty guidance on generative AI in teaching; members include Mike Holland (Vice Chancellor for Science Policy), Jennifer Seng (Deputy Chief Legal Officer), and faculty from across schools

bodies.name: Pitt AI Scholar-Teacher Alliance (PASTA)
bodies.scope: Cross-disciplinary community of faculty and staff convened by Professor Michael Madison and supported by the CIO; hosts monthly interdisciplinary discussions on AI across research and teaching

bodies.name: Pitt Cyber — Institute for Cyber Law, Policy, and Security (AI & Algorithmic Accountability program)
bodies.scope: Interdisciplinary institute (founded 2017) running policy research on AI governance, algorithmic registries, and AI procurement; houses the Pittsburgh Task Force on Public Algorithms

bodies.name: Office of the CIO / Pitt Digital — AI Enablement
bodies.scope: CIO Mark Henderson's organization, with Director of AI Enablement Joe Yun, operates the PittGPT platform and the campus-wide generative AI tool portfolio

governance_structure.summary: Pitt's AI governance is distributed across a governance hub (HAIL), a teaching-focused committee, a research ethics task force that produced a living 'Points to Consider' framework, a university-wide faculty alliance (PASTA), an algorithmic-accountability policy institute (Pitt Cyber), and an operational AI Enablement office inside the CIO organization. The model couples faculty deliberation with a dedicated AI enablement executive and formal policy working groups.

initiatives.title: Hub for AI and Data Science Leadership (HAIL) launch
initiatives.description: Pitt inaugurated HAIL in December 2025 as a university-wide hub unifying AI research, teaching, enterprise tooling, and regional workforce development under a 'Responsible Data Science' framing. Director Michael Colaresi leads the effort, which combines Claude for Education, PittGPT, Amazon Bedrock, and NVIDIA-powered GPU compute through the Center for Research Computing and Data.
initiatives.significance: Establishes a single operational hub for Pitt's AI strategy and ties enterprise AI deployment directly to regional workforce development in a way many peers have not attempted.

investment.funding_source: University budget with industry partner contributions (AWS, Anthropic, NVIDIA, Leidos)
investment.description: Aggregates existing campus AI spending and industry partnerships into a single hub; no standalone dollar figure disclosed.

collaborations.description: HAIL operationalizes Pitt's enterprise agreements with Anthropic/AWS for Claude for Education, NVIDIA for the Joint Center for AI and Intelligent Systems, and Leidos for CPACE.

tools_and_vendors.name: Claude for Education
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-wide LLM for teaching, research, and operations

tools_and_vendors.name: PittGPT
tools_and_vendors.role: Pitt-hosted private LLM for restricted data

tools_and_vendors.name: Amazon Bedrock
tools_and_vendors.role: Cloud AI platform underpinning PittGPT and custom applications

initiatives.policy_implications: HAIL positions Pitt as a model for coordinating enterprise AI deployment with formal governance and regional workforce strategy; other R1s will likely look to it for a reusable hub pattern.

initiatives.title: Claude for Education enterprise agreement with AWS integration
initiatives.description: On December 5, 2025, Pitt became the first university to integrate Claude for Education with AWS under an enterprise agreement. Faculty and staff received access immediately; students were on-boarded over winter recess via claude.ai with Pitt SSO. The agreement runs one year with no long-term lock-in; data remains private and is not used for training Anthropic models.
initiatives.significance: First-of-kind Claude+AWS integration establishes a new enterprise AI procurement pattern for higher education and validates multi-tool institutional AI stacks.

investment.description: Institution-wide license; exact pricing not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Joint deployment combining Anthropic's Claude 4.5 Sonnet models with AWS cloud infrastructure for custom teaching, research, and operational applications.

tools_and_vendors.name: Claude for Education
tools_and_vendors.role: Campus-wide generative AI assistant with Socratic-style learning mode

tools_and_vendors.name: Amazon Web Services
tools_and_vendors.role: Cloud infrastructure and Bedrock integration for custom Pitt applications

initiatives.policy_implications: CIO Mark Henderson emphasized use is voluntary and faculty retain full discretion, setting a precedent that enterprise AI deployments can scale without mandatory adoption.

initiatives.title: PittGPT private generative AI platform
initiatives.description: PittGPT is Pitt's in-house ChatGPT-like assistant designed for sensitive data, running on Amazon Bedrock under a closed-loop architecture. Led by Joe Yun, Director of AI Enablement, it is available to faculty and staff for work with restricted data and operates at a cost estimated around $10/user/year at full scale.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a mid-size R1 building and operating its own private enterprise LLM stack rather than relying solely on vendor tools for sensitive use cases.

investment.description: Internal build funded by Pitt Digital; operating cost estimated at roughly $10 per user per year.

collaborations.description: PittGPT runs on Amazon Bedrock with private model hosting so no Pitt data is shared with public model providers.

tools_and_vendors.name: PittGPT
tools_and_vendors.role: Private institutional LLM assistant for sensitive data

initiatives.policy_implications: Anchors the 'restricted data must stay in University GenAI Tools' rule in Pitt's acceptable use policy.

initiatives.title: Computational Pathology and AI Center of Excellence (CPACE) with $10M Leidos investment
initiatives.description: Leidos committed $10 million over five years to CPACE, which Pitt established in January 2024 within the School of Medicine to develop AI tools for disease detection across heart disease and cancer. CPACE operates a ScanVan, a mobile digitization unit, and the Leidos Computational Pathology Fellowship for clinical AI training.
initiatives.significance: Large, named industry gift into a dedicated clinical-AI center makes Pitt a recognized national leader in pathology AI and signals a scalable pattern for industry-funded applied AI research.

investment.description: Five-year $10M Leidos commitment to accelerate AI-powered disease detection at CPACE.

collaborations.description: Leidos funds AI tool development, a fellowship program, and the ScanVan slide-digitization initiative.

initiatives.title: NVIDIA Joint Center for AI and Intelligent Systems
initiatives.description: On October 14, 2024, Pitt signed an MOU with NVIDIA (alongside Carnegie Mellon) to establish a joint center focused on AI applications in clinical medicine and biomanufacturing, as part of NVIDIA's inaugural AI Tech Community in Pittsburgh. Governor Josh Shapiro, Chancellor Joan Gabel, and CMU President Farnam Jahanian co-announced.
initiatives.significance: Positions Pittsburgh as NVIDIA's first 'AI Tech Community' anchor and ties Pitt's clinical AI work to national-scale compute and hardware.

investment.description: Joint center with NVIDIA hardware, software, and collaboration; dollar value not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Multi-institution MOU anchoring NVIDIA's first AI Tech Community in Pittsburgh.

tools_and_vendors.name: NVIDIA A100/L40S GPU clusters
tools_and_vendors.role: Compute hardware for AI research through Pitt CRCD

initiatives.title: Acceptable Use of Generative AI Tools policy
initiatives.description: Pitt Digital publishes the institution-wide Acceptable Use of Generative AI Tools policy, which classifies GenAI systems by data sensitivity: University GenAI Tools (PittGPT, Claude for Education, Gemini, Copilot Chat) for restricted/private data, and public tools (ChatGPT, DeepSeek, Grammarly, Notion AI, etc.) for public data only.
initiatives.significance: Data-classification-based AUP is a template for other large R1s trying to balance faculty autonomy with privacy and compliance obligations.

investment.description: Policy authoring and enforcement embedded in existing compliance structures.

initiatives.policy_implications: Binds tool selection to data classification, establishes Vendor Security Risk Assessment as a precondition for any AI tool, and ties enforcement to Faculty/Staff/Student handbooks.

initiatives.title: Ad Hoc Committee on Gen AI in Research and Education report
initiatives.description: In March 2024 Pitt published 'Considerations for Responsible Use and Recommendations for Generative AI in Research and Education,' authored by Ravit Dotan, Lisa Parker, and John Radzilowicz. The report offers a 'Points to Consider' living framework, peer benchmarking, an eight-unit faculty survey, and publisher-policy analysis, recommending annual landscape reviews.
initiatives.significance: Pitt's framing of GenAI policy as a 'living framework' rather than a static document is a notable governance pattern that emphasizes recurring faculty deliberation.

investment.funding_source: Provost and Senior Vice Chancellor for Research offices
investment.description: Committee work, staff time, and peer benchmarking.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides the ethics scaffolding (positive-but-careful stance, humanistic perspective) underpinning Pitt's policy posture and continues to inform annual policy refresh.

initiatives.title: Online Bachelor of Science in Health Informatics with AI focus
initiatives.description: Pitt's School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences will launch the university's first fully online undergraduate degree in 2026, a BS in Health Informatics built around data analytics, programming, AI, and big-data systems. It is designed as an upper-division completion pathway at a reduced tuition, culminating in a clinical internship and capstone.
initiatives.significance: One of the first fully online accredited undergraduate programs nationally with a dedicated AI-in-healthcare focus; broadens access and targets a reported 83.4% annual growth in AI health-informatics jobs.

investment.funding_source: School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences
investment.description: Launch of a new online degree modality at reduced tuition; dollar figure not disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Signals willingness to create AI-native academic products and experiment with online-first degree pricing.

initiatives.title: AI Literacy course portfolio for Spring 2026
initiatives.description: Pitt's Spring 2026 course lineup introduces AI Literacy: Foundations for Critical Thinking and Informed Use (INFSCI 1499), a redesigned Computer Vision graduate course covering multimodal LLMs and agentic AI, LIS 2970 on emerging technologies for underserved communities, Foundations of AI for Business (undergrad and grad), and Data to Prediction: Machine Learning for Public Health (PUBHLT 0413).
initiatives.significance: Builds broad, cross-disciplinary AI literacy before formal requirements, positioning every school (Computing, Business, Public Health, Library Science) to introduce students to responsible and practical AI.
initiatives.policy_implications: Signals Pitt's intent to move AI literacy from optional to normative across the undergraduate experience.

initiatives.title: Pitt AI Scholar-Teacher Alliance (PASTA)
initiatives.description: PASTA emerged from an April 2023 University Senate plenary and has grown into a monthly interdisciplinary forum (first Tuesdays in Oakland) on AI research and teaching, convened by Professor of Law Michael Madison with CIO-level support. It connects seven centers including Pitt Cyber, the Center for Governance and Markets, and the Hub for AI and Data Science Leadership.
initiatives.significance: A grass-roots, faculty-led AI community that complements formal governance bodies and models peer-style sector-wide knowledge sharing within a single campus.

investment.funding_source: Office of the CIO and participating centers
investment.description: Convener time and meeting support; no dedicated budget disclosed.

collaborations.description: Seven-center on-campus alliance linking humanities, law, engineering, and policy programs.

initiatives.policy_implications: Keeps faculty voice central in policy evolution; counters top-down-only AI governance.

initiatives.title: Health Sciences and Sports Analytics Cloud Innovation Center with AWS
initiatives.description: Pitt partnered with AWS to open the first Cloud Innovation Center (CIC) on the US East Coast focused on health sciences and sports analytics, applying AI and cloud tools to biomedical, clinical, and performance data. The CIC collaborates with the School of Medicine and UPMC partners.
initiatives.significance: Adds Pitt to a small group of universities hosting AWS CICs and anchors AI-driven health and sports analytics research with industry-grade cloud tooling.

investment.description: AWS-sponsored CIC with cloud credits, staff, and project support; dollar value not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Cloud Innovation Center with AWS; UPMC participates on health data use cases.

tools_and_vendors.name: Amazon Web Services
tools_and_vendors.role: Cloud infrastructure and AI services for the CIC

initiatives.title: Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center Bridges-2 AI GPU upgrade
initiatives.description: PSC — a joint center of Pitt and Carnegie Mellon — won an NSF award to upgrade the Bridges-2 supercomputer with NVIDIA H100 GPUs, reinforcing its capacity to support the NAIRR pilot program. Bridges-2 and PSC's Neocortex (with two Cerebras CS-2 systems) are both NAIRR-allocated resources.
initiatives.significance: Keeps Pitt's shared compute infrastructure near the NSF national-scale AI frontier and provides broad access to researchers beyond the host institutions.

investment.description: $4.9M NSF award to upgrade Bridges-2 with NVIDIA H100 GPUs.

collaborations.description: Federally funded upgrade to a joint Pitt/CMU HPC resource that feeds NAIRR.

initiatives.title: Pitt BioForge AI-powered biomanufacturing center (Hazelwood Green)
initiatives.description: Pitt BioForge is a $250M biomanufacturing center at Hazelwood Green, anchored by ElevateBio and seeded by a $100M gift from the Richard King Mellon Foundation. AI, automation, and next-generation analytics are core to the facility's cell- and gene-therapy manufacturing platform.
initiatives.significance: Ties Pitt's AI workforce and research agenda to a $250M regional economic development project and signals AI's role in industrial biomanufacturing.

investment.funding_source: Pitt, ElevateBio, and $100M Richard King Mellon Foundation gift

collaborations.description: Regional development partnership linking biomanufacturing, AI training, and community access via HAIL's Life Sciences Career Pathways.

initiatives.policy_implications: Links AI workforce development to neighborhood-level access through Community Engagement Centers in Hazelwood and Homewood.

initiatives.title: Pitt Cyber AI & Algorithmic Accountability program
initiatives.description: Pitt Cyber (founded 2017 by David Hickton) runs a long-standing AI & Algorithmic Accountability program that has produced the Pittsburgh Task Force on Public Algorithms, publications on AI procurement and algorithmic registries, and AI governance inputs to local, state, federal, and multilateral bodies.
initiatives.significance: Provides Pitt with a serious AI policy research voice and creates institutional capacity to weigh in on state and federal AI regulation.

investment.description: Institute operating budget with external research grants.

collaborations.description: Policy research and advisory engagements on AI accountability.

initiatives.policy_implications: Gives Pitt direct input into regional and national AI procurement and accountability rules.

initiatives.title: International Conference on Health, AI & Technology
initiatives.description: Pitt and Carnegie Mellon hosted a three-day international conference October 19-21, 2025 with the Global Federation of Competitiveness Councils on AI's role in healthcare and economic growth. Chancellor Joan Gabel, CMU President Farnam Jahanian, and Gov. Josh Shapiro participated.
initiatives.significance: Elevates Pittsburgh's public profile as an AI hub and aligns university leadership with state government on health-AI strategy.

collaborations.description: Multi-party convening on health, AI, and economic growth.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Bridges-2 (PSC) and Neocortex (PSC); Pitt CRCD GPU cluster
computing_infrastructure.summary: Pitt co-operates the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center with Carnegie Mellon, hosting the petascale Bridges-2 system (recently upgraded with NVIDIA H100 GPUs under a $4.9M NSF award) and the AI-specialized Neocortex system with two Cerebras CS-2 engines. Both are NSF NAIRR-pilot resources. Pitt's own CRCD also runs NVIDIA A100/L40S GPU clusters supporting CUDA, TensorFlow, and PyTorch workflows for campus researchers.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: HAIL embeds AI workforce training in neighborhood-level Community Engagement Centers so residents in historically under-invested areas can access Life Sciences Career Pathways and AI vocational training.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Online BS in Health Informatics at reduced tuition
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Upper-division completion pathway in AI and health informatics offered fully online at reduced tuition, designed for working professionals and nontraditional learners.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Pittsburgh Task Force on Public Algorithms and publications on AI procurement, bias, and algorithmic registries shape equitable governmental use of AI at local and state levels.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: MLIS course focused on designing AI/immersive tech interventions for underserved communities.

regulatory_context.summary: Pennsylvania has moved early on state AI policy. Governor Josh Shapiro's Executive Order 2023-19 established a Generative AI Governing Board for state government and 10 core values guiding AI use. The Commonwealth ran a first-in-the-nation ChatGPT Enterprise pilot in 2024 and signed a 2025 Cooperative Agreement with the University of Pennsylvania for AI advising services. A statewide Artificial Intelligence Policy took effect January 13, 2026. The 2026-27 budget proposes four reforms targeting AI companion bots and age verification. Pennsylvania was ranked top-3 for government AI readiness by Code for America in July 2025. Pitt operates within this context but is not part of a university system with its own AI policy.

controversies_and_litigation.summary: The Pitt-AWS/Anthropic deal to integrate AI into university academics drew faculty debate in the student press, with some faculty publicly questioning pace, vendor dependence, and academic norms around generative AI.

narrative_summary: The University of Pittsburgh has assembled one of the most structurally complete AI postures among large public R1s without a university-system layer. It couples a dedicated executive role (Joe Yun as Director of AI Enablement inside the CIO organization) with a newly launched governance hub (HAIL, December 2025, under Director Michael Colaresi) and an applied clinical-AI flagship (CPACE, funded by a $10M Leidos gift). The enterprise AI stack is unusually deep for a public university: PittGPT is a private, Bedrock-hosted institutional LLM, and Pitt became the first university to deploy Claude for Education integrated with AWS in December 2025, with Google Gemini, NotebookLM, and Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat also available campus-wide. The research infrastructure rests on the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center (joint with Carnegie Mellon), which operates the NAIRR-pilot Bridges-2 and Neocortex systems, recently upgraded with NVIDIA H100 GPUs through a $4.9M NSF award. NVIDIA named Pittsburgh its inaugural AI Tech Community anchor in October 2024, further pulling federal and industry attention. Governance is distributed and deliberative: a March 2024 Ad Hoc Committee report set a 'Points to Consider' living framework, Pitt Cyber runs a long-standing AI and Algorithmic Accountability program, and PASTA convenes cross-disciplinary faculty monthly. The Acceptable Use of Generative AI Tools policy uses a comprehensive data-classification model that permits experimentation while requiring Vendor Security Risk Assessment for sensitive data. On the teaching side, Pitt is adding AI literacy, AI for business, and machine-learning-for-public-health courses for Spring 2026 and launching an AI-focused online BS in Health Informatics in 2026. Regionally, Pitt BioForge (a $250M biomanufacturing facility seeded by a $100M Mellon gift and partnered with ElevateBio) and HAIL's Community Engagement Centers in Hazelwood and Homewood tie AI directly to local workforce development. Faculty debate over the pace of vendor integration is live but has not become litigation. The principal gaps in Pitt's posture are the absence of a single published multi-year AI strategy document and limited public evidence of predictive-analytics retention or campus-wide syllabus-level policy mandates.
